plugin_desc: Zyxel-specific SNMP collection for switches (ZYXEL private MIB, enterprise 890). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects Zyxel private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization (direct percentage) and hardware temperature sensors. Zyxel exposes fan as raw RPM (not a state) and this switch line has no memory-utilization or power-supply-state OID, so those health items are not collected (N/A).
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the Zyxel device control plane (ZYXEL private MIB), normalized to a 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the temperature reading of each Zyxel hardware sensor (ZYXEL private MIB temperature table), in degrees Celsius. Abnormally high temperatures may indicate fan failure, blocked airflow, or excessive ambient heat, and can trigger thermal protection or hardware damage if left unaddressed.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
